- [ ] tests added / passed
- [ ] release document entry (if new feature or API change)

As I exposed in #14149 , this PR add some optional arguments to the colorbar's title to define its location, orientation and alignment. I also modified the padding input so it can be a tuple with four values (top, right, bottom and left sides).

I'm using the [colorbar_log example](https://docs.bokeh.org/en/latest/docs/examples/basic/annotations/colorbar_log.html) as a test.

## Example 1
```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(0, 0, 0, 10), title='Title', title_location='above')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "right")
```
![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4f4a531f-e067-4265-8466-d8e1ac002620)

## Example 2

```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(10, 0, 0, 0), title='Title', title_location='below', title_text_halign='center')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "below")
```

![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3fa38634-aa25-4c34-82b7-1d1027138533)

## Example 3

```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(0, 0, 0, 10), title='Title', title_location='right', title_orientation='horizontal', title_text_valign='middle')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "right")
```

![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5c3e3c56-5931-4615-a0e8-d3a68db38bd8)

I'm still dealing with some combinations and the idea is to preserve the old behavior if the new arguments are omitted, so it's work in progress.

- [ ] tests added / passed
- [ ] release document entry (if new feature or API change)

As I exposed in #14149 , this PR add some optional arguments to the colorbar's title to define its location, orientation and alignment. I also modified the padding input so it can be a tuple with four values (top, right, bottom and left sides).

I'm using the [colorbar_log example](https://docs.bokeh.org/en/latest/docs/examples/basic/annotations/colorbar_log.html) as a test.

## Example 1
```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(0, 0, 0, 10), title='Title', title_location='above')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "right")
```
![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4f4a531f-e067-4265-8466-d8e1ac002620)

## Example 2

```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(10, 0, 0, 0), title='Title', title_location='below', title_text_halign='center')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "below")
```

![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3fa38634-aa25-4c34-82b7-1d1027138533)

## Example 3

```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(0, 0, 0, 10), title='Title', title_location='right', title_orientation='horizontal', title_text_valign='middle')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "right")
```

![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5c3e3c56-5931-4615-a0e8-d3a68db38bd8)

I'm still dealing with some combinations and the idea is to preserve the old behavior if the new arguments are omitted, so it's work in progress.

- [ ] tests added / passed
- [ ] release document entry (if new feature or API change)

As I exposed in #14149 , this PR add some optional arguments to the colorbar's title to define its location, orientation and alignment. I also modified the padding input so it can be a tuple with four values (top, right, bottom and left sides).

I'm using the [colorbar_log example](https://docs.bokeh.org/en/latest/docs/examples/basic/annotations/colorbar_log.html) as a test.

## Example 1
```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(0, 0, 0, 10), title='Title', title_location='above')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "right")
```
![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4f4a531f-e067-4265-8466-d8e1ac002620)

## Example 2

```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(10, 0, 0, 0), title='Title', title_location='below', title_text_halign='center')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "below")
```

![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3fa38634-aa25-4c34-82b7-1d1027138533)

## Example 3

```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(0, 0, 0, 10), title='Title', title_location='right', title_orientation='horizontal', title_text_valign='middle')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "right")
```

![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5c3e3c56-5931-4615-a0e8-d3a68db38bd8)

I'm still dealing with some combinations and the idea is to preserve the old behavior if the new arguments are omitted, so it's work in progress.

As I exposed in #14149 , this PR add some optional arguments to the colorbar's title to define its location, orientation and alignment. I also modified the padding input so it can be a tuple with four values (top, right, bottom and left sides).

I'm using the [colorbar_log example](https://docs.bokeh.org/en/latest/docs/examples/basic/annotations/colorbar_log.html) as a test.

## Example 1
```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(0, 0, 0, 10), title='Title', title_location='above')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "right")
```
![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4f4a531f-e067-4265-8466-d8e1ac002620)

## Example 2

```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(10, 0, 0, 0), title='Title', title_location='below', title_text_halign='center')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "below")
```

![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3fa38634-aa25-4c34-82b7-1d1027138533)

## Example 3

```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(0, 0, 0, 10), title='Title', title_location='right', title_orientation='horizontal', title_text_valign='middle')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "right")
```

![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5c3e3c56-5931-4615-a0e8-d3a68db38bd8)

I'm still dealing with some combinations and the idea is to preserve the old behavior if the new arguments are omitted, so it's work in progress.

As I exposed in #14149 , this PR add some optional arguments to the colorbar's title to define its location, orientation and alignment. I also modified the padding input so it can be a tuple with four values (top, right, bottom and left sides).

I'm using the [colorbar_log example](https://docs.bokeh.org/en/latest/docs/examples/basic/annotations/colorbar_log.html) as a test.

## Example 1
```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(0, 0, 0, 10), title='Title', title_location='above')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "right")
```
![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4f4a531f-e067-4265-8466-d8e1ac002620)

## Example 2

```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(10, 0, 0, 0), title='Title', title_location='below', title_text_halign='center')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "below")
```

![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3fa38634-aa25-4c34-82b7-1d1027138533)

## Example 3

```python
color_bar = r.construct_color_bar(padding=(0, 0, 0, 10), title='Title', title_location='right', title_orientation='horizontal', title_text_valign='middle')
plot.add_layout(color_bar, "right")
```

![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5c3e3c56-5931-4615-a0e8-d3a68db38bd8)

I'm still dealing with some combinations and the idea is to preserve the old behavior if the new arguments are omitted, so it's work in progress.

There are 3 Windows' tests that failed, but it seems is not because of the changes I did 😬 .

## Discussion

I have added 4 new parameters to `ColorBar` to change the title location, orientation, horizontal and vertical alignment.

```python
title_orientation = String("auto", help="""
Wheter the color bar's title should be oriented upward,
downward or horizontal.
""")

title_location = Either(Enum(Location), Auto, default="auto", help="""
Specifies on which side of the color bar the title will be located.
""")

title_text_halign = Enum(HAlign, default="left", help="""
Specifies where to align color bar's title horizontally.
""")

title_text_valign = Enum(VerticalAlign, default="bottom", help="""
Specifies where to align color bar's title vertically.
""")
```

I've been thinking that maybe it could be reduced to 3 or even to 2. I'd like to read your opinions

### ```title_orientation```

This parameter allows you to write the title in different orientations: upward, downward and horizontal.

![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b2b54b3d-da8b-414e-88a8-451a4af0e2df)
_Figure 1: Example of the three available title orientations_

However, I'm not sure if this function is really neccessary, and maybe it is better to use an automatic behaviour. Currently, the title orientation behaves as follows when is omitted:
- If the colorbar is horizontal, the title is horizontal regardless of the title's location.
- If the color bar is vertical and the title's position is above or below it, the title will be horizontal. However, if the title is positioned to the left of the color bar, it will be oriented upward, and if it is to the right, it will be oriented downward.

![imagen](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/256f48b7-ca50-4a71-8b20-cd67465e49a5)
_Figure 2: Automatic behavior of the title orientation depeding on the colorbar position and title location_

On the other hand, I'm using two values that are not listed in the typical enumerations used in Bokeh (upward and downward), I don't know if we should add these values for something that specific. I'm inclined to use always the automatic behavior. What do you think?

### ```title_text_halign``` and ```title_text_valign```

I added these two parameters because I noticed that the original parameter ```title_text_align``` is not being passed properly to the `Title` object 

```diff
  protected _apply_title_properties(): void {
    const attrs: Partial<Title.Attrs> = {
      text: this.model.title ?? "",
      standoff: this.model.title_standoff,
+     align: this.model.title_text_halign,
+     vertical_align: this.model.title_text_valign,
      // TODO: this needs strict typing
      ...mixins.attrs_of(this.model, "title_", mixins.Text, false),
    }
    this._title.setv(attrs)
  }
```
I see two options:
1. We could deprecate the ```title_text_align``` parameter and mantain the explicit horizontal and vertical alignment, or
2. We could change (fix) the ```title_text_align``` parameter in order to capture the horizontal and vertical alignment in one single parameter. That would require, I think, to use a definition like "above = left", "middle = center" and "below = right".

## Change padding parameter

In addition, I changed the ```padding``` parameter so it can be a single value (as it is now) or a tuple of four numbers, so the user can indicate different padding values for the top, right, bottom and left sides.

## Default behavior

The default values were chosen in order to maintain the current behavior, so the users should not see any difference if they update the Bokeh library.

Again, I'd like to know your thoughs. As @mattpap mentioned in #14149 this could be implemented for the `ScaleBar` and maybe the `Legend` models, but I think it is better to discuss the behavior before to implement it in other models.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/comments/2475308922/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
